description: 92-51: There is no mention of the 3/4 vote by Presbytery to approve previous experience for an internship.
There is no indication that the requirements of BCO § 18-2 were met.
The purpose of the call is not stated, nor found in order, nor is it appended.
There is no indication that the requirements of BCO § 18-2 were met.
Permanent Committee Reports, B, MNA: there is no correspondence or explanation for the severance between the teaching elder and his church, nor is his subsequent status within presbytery defined.
Exception from 20th GA: Presbytery dividing the Court to hear multiple ordination sermons.
response: Central Georgia Presbytery receives as information the exception of substance and respectfully responds that though it disagrees with the exception for practical reasons, Presbytery will discontinue its practice of dividing the Court to hear multiple ordination sermons until such time a sit becomes an allowable practice. Thank you for calling this to our attention.

description: p. 8: Presbytery cannot divide to hear ordination sermons.
p. 11: Presbytery dissolved the pastoral relationship with no evidence of congregational concurrence.
p. 13, 15: The call to a non-PCA work was approved with no evidence that the assurances of BCO § 8-7 were received.
p. 20: Presbytery erected a commission which included non-members of the Court (as opposed to inviting ones to participate as visiting brothers).
p. 23: a commission was appointed to license a man with no evidence that he had presented a sermon to Presbytery or been examined orally before Presbytery.
there is no up to date directory, roll of presbytery or list of candidates under care and licentiates, nor is there an up to date copy of the standing rules.
p. 316: There is no indication recorded of a teaching elder's transfer.
pp. 2,3: There is no indication of the nature of complaints that are mentioned in the Stated Clerk's report.
Exception from 20th GA: The full text of a pastoral call is not recorded or referenced and there is no indication of congregational action (three instances).
response: Three of the exceptions are the same in nature with the Minutes for May 1, 1991, September 24, 1991 and January 28, 1992 in that the full text of a pastoral call is not recorded or referenced and there is no indication of congregational action. I, as the Stated Clerk, express my apology for not including a copy of the call in BCO 20-6, but we haven't done it that way since I've been clerk (since 1976) and it has never been called to our attention. We as a Presbytery promise to reference all calls in the future assuring you that all calls that have previously been approved have been reviewed by a committee of Presbytery and approved by Presbytery. In all of the three cases cited by your committee, we assumed if a call was presented by the particular church calling the particular man it was certainly by congregational action or Sessional action as the case may be. We will comply.

description: Page 152 is missing.
Exception from 20th GA: Erection of a Commission to approve minutes which did not report its action to the next Presbytery Meeting.
response: The Presbytery of Grace at its September 8, 1992 Meeting agrees with the exception taken to its May 8, 1990 Minutes concerning the erection of a Commission to approve those May 8, 1990 Minutes which did not report its action to the next Presbytery Meeting. we pledge to be more diligent in the use of such commissions in the future.

description: There is no mention of approval of internship for a man being ordained.
p. 14: The request to divest a minister without censure was handled at the initial meeting rather than at the second meeting.
p. 18: There is no mention of authorization to proclaim the Reformed faith or validation of the area of ministry.

Exception from 19th GA: (1) Stated Spring Meeting of March 10-11, 1989: Violation of BCO 21-5 in that the individual who preached at an installation service was not a member of or invited by the Presbytery; violation of BCO 5-9 in that ruling elders were elected five weeks before the church was organized. (2) Stated Fall Meeting of September 14-15, 1990: Violation of BCO 21-6 and 21-10 in that the Commission addressed the installation questions to the congregation rather than the session at the installation of an Assistant Pastor.
response: (1) The Commission did not follow the BCO in the matter, so Presbytery will instruct future commissions to do so. (2) This Commission failed to follow the BCO, so Presbytery will try to instruct future commissions ahead of time. On motion it was determined to make the above Presbytery's answers to the 20th General Assembly.

description: Pages 259-266 are missing: the minutes indicate they contain reports presented and acted upon.
p. 274, # 92-86: There is no record of the required 4/5 vote of the congregation.
p. 293, # 93-9: A teaching elder was received with no record of his being examined touching his views in theology, etc.

Exception from 20th GA: (1) November 8-9,1991: Page 210, 91-154 Presbytery failed to record in its minutes the reasons why it considers this work to be a valid Christian ministry when the source of the call came from a source other than a church in the PCA. (2) March 20-21, 1992: Page 233, 92-33: The minutes fail to record whether Presbytery ascertained congregations concurrence with a teaching elder's request for dissolution of the pastoral relationship.
response: Exception 1 Response: Presbytery agrees with the exception in that, technically, it should have recorded the assumption that it considered the call from a non-PCA church as coming from a 'valid Christian ministry.' However, presbytery's action to require the stipulation of agreement with BCO 8-7 and its next action #91-155 to ordain Holmes as an evangelist laboring out of bounds, achieves the same purpose, while protecting the individual and the presbytery. Exception 2 Response: The phrase 'by mutual agreement' indicates that presbytery had in fact ascertained congregational concurrence in the dissolution. The Care of Churches Committee had in hand minutes of a duly called congregational meeting, moderated by an honorably retired PCA teaching elder, during which the congregation acted to accept the pastor's resignation which had been submitted to the session. The matter came to the attention of the committee through the action of the congregation at the direction of the session.

description: 6-A: There is no record that, when the pastor was allowed to labor in a church outside the bounds of presbytery, he was guaranteed full freedom to teach and practice the Reformed faith.
6-A: The minutes are not clear as to the nature of the questions concerning the Sabbath, nor how his questions related to his subscription vows.
6E-4: A ruling elder was being allowed to continue in office who does not hold to infant baptism.
6: The Care Committee did not report on the ruling elder who does not hold to infant baptism.
3-C: The ruling elder who does not hold to infant baptism was still on the session.
p. 1: The purpose of the called meeting is not stated.
There is no record that a teaching elder signed the ministerial obligation.
There is no copy of the call appended to the minutes.
p. 310: there is no clear statement that a candidate is either a member of a church of the presbytery or that he has the endorsement of a session.
p. 317: There is no notation of congregational approval of the procedure of a commission replacing the session.

Exception from 19th and 20th GA: Multiple exceptions including (1) no evidence Calvary Presbytery had previously approved internship; (2) no record of required 3/4 vote; (3) private communion contrary to Standards; (4) no directory, roll or list of candidates and licentiates for three years; (5) response to 19th GA exception re use of commissions not approved as satisfactory.
response: 1. Responses to Jan 10-11, 1992 Minutes: (a) Calvary Presbytery did not previously approve Mr. Stone's internship. (b) Illiana apologizes for not recording a specific vote on either motion. On voice vote there were no votes against and no abstentions to either motion. (c) Illiana Presbytery acknowledges a poor choice of words in referring to this as 'private Communion', as Illiana is aware that true private communion is contrary to the nature of the sacrament. Illiana submits that at least two elders (including one TE) along with members of the Akin family were present at such communion. 2. Response to General Exception: (directory) As can be seen from Ms. Brenda Hoyt's letter of May 4, 1992, the office of the Stated Clerk of the PCA, indicated they received and have on file Illiana's Directory of 1/12/91. (commissions exception) Illiana will not in the future divide itself for the purpose of examining candidates, unless the standards of the PCA are amended to allow such division.

description: There is no record that presbytery followed its bylaws (p. 7) and the BCO (§ 13-9, b.; § 40-3) in examining sessional records.
Exception from 20th GA: General: There is no mention of an annual report of candidates for the ministry under the presbytery's care. (This presbytery has been cited for this previously.)
response: A motion was adopted that Presbytery respectfully agrees with the exception of substance concerning no list or mention of an annual report of Candidates under care as required by BCO 18-6 and promised to be more careful and to improve its reporting in the future.

description: Exception from 20th GA: April 10, 1992: Reference 92.2.018 -- Presbytery cannot entrust an ordination examination to a commission.
response: Mid-America Presbytery respectfully concurs with the Assembly's exception. The next business day after the meeting in question, the Stated Clerk of Presbytery researched the matter, and informed the Chairman of the Candidates and Credentials Committee, which had been clothed as a Commission in the matter, that the Commission could not be entrusted with ordination examination. The Commission never met in this capacity; no examination by Commission was conducted.

description: Exception from 20th GA: [Response approved as satisfactory; specific exception text not reproduced in these minutes].
response: [Response approved as satisfactory; specific response text not reproduced in minutes beyond noting approval]

description: Exception from 20th GA: Page 77 There were no letters of recommendation for men taken under care from their Sessions, nor was there any evidence that they had been members of the Church for at least six months. Page 77 There was no evidence that either candidate for licensure had produced a written sermon. There were no reports from interns.
response: The Minutes will be corrected to show that Chris Polski and John Puryear had been members of their respective Churches for six months prior to coming Under Care; and that their letters of recommendation shall be included in the Documentary file of Presbytery. Paul Frick and Cooper McWhirter were assigned, and did produce written sermons which were preached before Presbytery; and the Minutes shall be corrected to so indicate. And, from this review on we shall tally and show in our Minutes 'reports' and 'no reports' of Interns. Presbytery requires quarterly reports of Interns now in conformity with the BCO.

description: p. 261, # 7: There is no record of a teaching elder signing the ministerial obligation.
p. 256, item G: There is no record of the two complaints, one of which was sustained, and one of which was not.

Exception from 20th GA: (1) September 28, 1991: page 217, no copy of a commission report is appended to or included in the minutes. (2) November 23, 1991: page 234, Neither the commission's report nor its minutes were included either in the minutes of presbytery or appended. (3) February 15, 1992: page 247, Neither the commission's report nor its minutes were included either in the minutes of the presbytery or appended.
response: It was moved and carried to adopt the following as the response of the Presbytery to the exceptions for the minutes of 9/18/91, 11/23/91, and 2/15/92. These are separate instances of the same exception, i.e. failure to include in the Presbytery's minutes, or append to them, the minutes or reports of a commission. ... [Presbytery argued the practice is not truly required for routine ordination commissions and that any citation of RAO 14-10-f-2 was erroneous.] Presbytery notes the expressed intention of its clerks to record these types of commissions in a manner more clearly in accordance with the specific requirements of the BCO and rules of RAO. Approved by Presbytery September 19,1992.

description: There is no annual report of candidates.
p. 5: There is no record of examination on church government.
Exception from 20th GA: December 7, 1991, p. 2, 53-1-4 ff. The minutes of a session's executive session were read in open court, which is impermissible.
response: New River Presbytery agrees with the exception and promises to be more careful in the future.

description: p. 22, # 17 d: The minutes do not show that a candidate's sermon was preached before the whole presbytery as part of his ordination examination.
p. 22 of 27, line 38: There is no record of a teaching elder being required to sign the Book of Obligation.

Attachment # 2, line 7, item # 3: The commission met and acted without a quorum. (p. 13 of 26, line 12: The minutes for May 10-11, 1991 show that the same problem was cited last year and occurs again this year.)
p. 20, # 23, 1: There is no record of the presbytery having received testimonials of a candidate's Christian character and promise of usefulness.
p. 21 of 21, item 24.5: The minutes record no reasons for presbytery not having taken action on a retired teaching elder's divorce.

Exception from 20th GA: (1) page 27, #18 A Commission to install must have at least two teaching elders and two ruling elders present. This commission had only one teaching elder. (2) page 27, #18 there is no record of a sermon being preached as part of the ordination exam. (3) page 27, #18 The commission appointed a member to itself. (4) page 36, #22.2 there is no record of a sermon being preached as part of the trials for ordination, or that the candidate met the educational requirements or the original languages requirement. [Response appropriate, but exceptions continued in practice.]
response: (1) Presbytery agrees with the exception and promises to be more careful in the future. Presbytery hereby reminds its commissions that they may not act without a lawful quorum. (2) Presbytery is unable to identify this exception to the Minutes from the reference cited. (3) Presbytery agrees with the exception and corrects the record by noting that the Moderator, in accordance with its Standing Rules, appointed the member to the commission, not the commission itself. (4) Presbytery agrees with the exception and corrects its records by noting that the candidate did preach before presbytery at its January 11-12, 1991 Stated Meeting on Matthew 2:1-12 as part of his trials for licensure. Further, Presbytery accepted the candidates degrees of Master of Divinity from Westminster Theological Seminary and Master of Theology in Historical Theology from Princeton Theological Seminary as fulfilling the requirements of BCO 21-4. Presbytery failed to approve these two items by separate acts as part of the ordination trials and promises to be more careful in the future.

description: p. 3, H 9: A teaching elder was erased from the roll of presbytery by BCO § 46-2, instead of BCO § 34-10; § 13-2.
p. 8, H 13: The quorum of a commission was improperly constituted.
92-38, p. 14: There is no 3/4 vote recorded to approve previous experience in lieu of internship.
92-38, p. 15: There is no record of BCO § 19-4 being implemented.
92-58, p. 20: The purpose of the called meeting is not recorded.
92-68, p. 25: The first reading of amendments to the standing rules is in violation of SR § 13-2, requiring this to be done at stated meetings only.
92-68, p. 26: Amendments made to the minutes of the April 24-25, 1992 meeting are not recorded.
92-90, p. 35: The second ratifying vote to amend the standing rules is invalid because of the above (92-68, p. 25).

Exception from 20th GA: (1) Paragraph 4 There is no report of the commission in the minutes of the presbytery. (2) Paragraph 4 There is no record that the commission was organized according to BCO § 15-2.
response: The particular problem with the minutes of North Georgia Presbytery for October 15, 1991, arises from the fact that the session of the Chapel Woods Presbyterian Church was not familiar with the PCA Book of Church Order and acted according to the rules of the PCUS where the session installed assistant ministers. When presbytery discovered this, the session had already installed Teaching Elder Paul Owens. Rather than ask that this be done over, presbytery took the action recorded in the minutes noting the fact that we were aware of the irregularity. We were aware of the fact that the commission was not set up according to BCO 15-1 and no commission report was in the minutes because there was no presbytery commission. Presbytery's error was made in the Called Meeting of June 1, 1991, when no commission of presbytery was set up to install Mr. Owens. For this oversight and the resulting confusion we apologize. We trust that from this we have learned always to set up a commission when a man is received. Approved by the Presbytery of North Georgia January 16,1993.

description: 2707, p. 11: There are no reports on interns.
p. 8, item 7: There is no record that the church concurred with the resignation of the teaching elder.

Exception from 20th GA: (a) January 11,1991, p. 8, 4a & b: There is no record of the congregation's concurrence with the proposed dissolution of pastoral relationships. (b) April 26-27,1991: p. 19, item 2 There is no record that a teaching elder called to a non PCA work was granted full freedom to maintain and teach the doctrine of our church. (c) April 26-27, 1991: p. 24, item 10 there is no record that the candidate for ordination submitted the required theological and exegetical papers, or that the same were approved, nor of the required sermon being preached. (d) April 26-27,1991: p. 25, item 1 There is no record of the congregation's concurrence with the proposed dissolution of pastoral relationships.
response: (a) Presbytery acknowledges the error. An examination of its records indicates that in a duly called meeting of the congregation, the congregation did in fact vote to concur in both resignations. Presbytery stands corrected and will seek to take greater care in the reporting of such resignations in the future. (b) Presbytery acknowledges the error. as a matter of fact, that issue was discussed at length in the Presbytery's Credentials Committee. Presbytery stands corrected and promises to take greater care in such cases in the future. (c) Presbytery acknowledges the error. An examination of Presbytery records indicates that the required papers had been submitted and were approved and that the required sermon had been preached and approved as part of his examination for licensure. The same should have been reported in the minutes. (d) Presbytery acknowledges the error. An examination of Presbytery records indicates that the congregation, in a duly called meeting did, in fact, concur in the resignation of TE Reaves. Presbytery stands corrected and will seek to take greater care in the reporting of such resignations in the future.

description: Women should not be on an executive committee in what appears to be a ruling capacity.

Exception from 20th GA exceptions #1, 2, 4, and 5: (1) May 11,1991, p.1, #7, There are no minutes of the commission to ordain in the appendix. (2) p. 2, #13-D, There is no record of the examination of a teaching elder as per BCO 13-6. (4) January 11, 1992, p. 6, 18 - The minutes do not identify areas of the examination as required by BCO 21-4. (5) p. 6, #16 - The minutes do not reflect either presbytery of the committee as hearing or approving the licensure sermon.
response: (1) Presbytery agrees that it was in error and has been attempting to rectify such matters since then. (2) TE David Kang had already been admitted to Presbytery at the previous meeting (see minutes of March 9,1991). (4) We understand this exception now to be moot due to General Assembly action to permit summary recording of these examinations. (5) Presbytery acknowledges the omission and responds by way of its Licensure Committee that said sermon was indeed heard by that committee.

description: Exception from 20th GA exception #3: September 14,1991, p. 3, #17-E - The minutes do not reflect congregational assent to the dissolution of pastoral relations. [Response not approved as it was incorrect; the presbytery clerk's pending recommendation to presbytery would resolve this difficulty.]
response: The Presbytery has searched diligently and has been unable to find this reference in its minutes of September 14,1991.

description: Exception from 20th GA: The minutes for 2/9/91 and 5/14/91 showed no reports from interns and, in general, no reports given from candidates under care.
response: (i) It was M/A that the Presbytery agrees that we have failed on occasion to provide time for reports of interns. We will attempt, in the future, to comply with this requirement. (ii) It was moved that the Presbytery agrees that we have not provided reports from candidates under care. Each candidate will be reported on to Presbytery in the future.

description: SR 1.055: 'Presbytery committees may be composed of any members in good standing of any congregation,' is in violation of BCO § 7-2, § 9-7.
SR 1.031, H 2 has a provision that mission churches may be governed by committees.
SR 1.022 allows a called meeting of presbytery by representatives of two churches, contrary to BCO § 13-11.
Reports as a whole are approved but not appended nor spread upon the minutes.
p. 3, # 19: A judicial commission was established in the absence of charges to deal with a situation in a local church; no quorum is specified, nor was a convenor named.
p. 2, # 18: The judicial commission's judgment was approved and indicated to be in the addendum, but it was not included.
p. 3, # 18: A letter of recommendation regarding the teaching elder, sustaining him in theology and the sacraments was to be written, but there was no record of his examination.
p. 3, # 38-24, § 4: The standing rules were amended with no indication that proper procedure was followed.
p. 4, # 38-24, § 8: Exceptions to session minutes must be specified.
p. 4, # 38-31, § 2: A commission was established without a quorum and no task was specified.

p. 4, # 38-33, § 3 f.: An individual's ordination was approved, but there was no indication that his internship was completed or waived, nor that he was a candidate prior to the meeting, nor that he preached a sermon.

Exceptions from previous General Assemblies: [responses too unclear to allow proper evaluation of their merits]. The presbytery is directed to refine these responses at their next stated meeting and report to the 22nd General Assembly.
response: M/S/A that presbytery accept the corrections to presbytery minutes as outlined by Bob Steadman. (see minutes April 22-23, 1993 Stated Meeting, p. 2, item 38-24, § 5). Note: No specifics were submitted to the General Assembly.

description: Exception from 20th GA: General: No minutes record the annual reports from candidates or ministers laboring out of bounds.
response: It was moved, seconded and passed that the Presbytery concur with the judgment of the 20th General Assembly that the Presbytery failed to record the reception of the annual reports of candidates and ministers laboring outside the bounds of Presbytery; and that the Stated Clerk inform the General Assembly that it is our intention not only to receive each of these reports this year, but also to record their reception and disposition.

description: The minutes of two churches were not received for review.
p. 3, Treasurer's Report: a motion was made by a non-member of the court.
p. 4: A commission reported, but its minutes were neither included nor appended.
p. 5: The minutes of three commissions were not appended.
p. 6: There is no record of congregational concurrence in the resignation.
p. 5: A teaching elder was suspended from the sacraments (indefinitely) and from office (indefinitely) without any explanation, process or confession.
pp. 5,7: A non-member of the court made motions.
p. 6: There was immediate ordination of an evangelist without record of compliance with BCO § 21-5, 1, or BCO § 21-7: the laying on of hands, exhortations, etc.
92-5-6, p. 1: The moderator did not establish the size or quorum of the commission.
Exception from 20th GA: October 22, 1991: Pages 91-6-15 & 91-6-25 The minutes of a Called Meeting for September 23,1991 are referred to but were not submitted to the General Assembly for review.
response: In response to exceptions, the minutes that were omitted have been submitted for review.

description: There was no separate directory or roll of Presbytery.
p. 4, 10-D, 4: There is no record of a call from the church. Note: an assistant pastor is called by the session; an associate pastor is called by the congregation.
p. 4, 9-B, 2: There is no record of a call from the church. There is no record of congregational vote for an assistant pastor to become the senior pastor.

p. 5, 9-B, 6: A non-PCA ordained minister was serving as a stated supply. He was examined as to his views, but in order for him to be licensed, the BCO § 19-2 requires that he be examined in basic knowledge as outlined.
92-18, # 6: There is no action taken regarding a teaching elder who has been without call for more than five years.
92-37, # 13: There is no action taken regarding teaching elders who have been without call for more than three years.

Exception from 20th GA: Minutes of the September 26-27, 1991 Stated Meeting, p. 91-24, Section 20 h: That a commission was appointed without evidence in the minutes of there being a request from the church that they do so and there is no provision for a Session pro tempore. Section 20 i: Same exception. General: That there is no annual report of candidates under care.
response: The exception is acknowledged. [Various details about Rocky Mountain Presbytery handling the commission matter.] For the annual candidates report: the exception is acknowledged. It was thought that showing the list of candidates under care in the Presbytery Directory met the BCO requirement; however, it is clear that a written report from each candidate (and from his instructors) should be obtained. A change in the Standing Rules to make this a clear responsibility of the Christian Education Committee is being requested.

description: Exception from 20th GA: (1) February 16, 1991, p. 160: The President and members at large from the Women in the Church may be advisors of presbytery committees, but they may not be regular members (1 Timothy 2:11-12). (2) September 21, 1991, p. 179: The appointment of a moderator for a session should include an indication of the request or consent of the session (BCO § 12-3).
response: (1) Presbytery respectfully disagrees with the exception and refers it back to the Assembly for action, noting that Presbytery does not believe membership on a Presbytery committee gives authority or makes anyone a teacher, but is a place of service. (2) M/S/C on page 179, under John Woll, change 'TE Harold Kelley shall serve as Moderator of the session with the consent of the New Covenant Fellowship Session.'

description: The presbytery did not include commission reports of ordination and/or installation of pastors. Details of calls were not included in the minutes.
p. 5, Ministerial Committee: There was no mention of the congregation's response to their pastor's resignation.
20.118: There is no intern report included in the minutes.
20.310: The request for stated supply relationship should include a request from the session.
20.439: The previous minutes give licensure status of a person now listed as an intern, but there is no record of his internship being approved.

Exception from 20th GA (and 16th and 18th GA): (1) Exception of substance noted page 5, item 3 regarding the dissolution of pastoral relations - congregation did approve the dissolution but it was not included in the minutes. (2) Citing for failure to respond to exceptions in 1987 and 1989 minutes.
response: Exception 1 response: Please note that the congregation did approve the dissolution and that it was a matter of failing to include this in the minutes and this will be corrected. Exception 2 response: It was reported that no response was made and yet the response itself is in the text of the 1992 minutes. This response was sent to the GA Stated Clerk and obviously given to the Review and Control Committee.
